Why are you interested in this role?: What pulls me to this role is that I genuinely enjoy being the reliable center of a complex operation—the person who keeps the logistics, the scheduling, and the communication running so a hiring process feels effortless to everyone in it. At InGenius Prep I coordinate logistics and scheduling for 20+ programs and I'm the primary point of contact for stakeholders, and the part I like most isn't just executing—it's finding the process improvement underneath, the way I built the documentation that cut onboarding time 75%. A Recruiting Coordinator role is that instinct made into a job.
Why do you want to work at Samsara?: Two reasons. First, the work fits how I'm wired: I'm extremely organized—my supervisor called me the most organized person he's ever worked with—and I like coordination that has real stakes, like getting candidates and interviewers a seamless experience. Second, I want to deepen my work in talent and recruiting operations, and Samsara is scaling fast with serious technical hiring, which is exactly the high-complexity environment where good coordination matters most. I have hands-on Greenhouse experience and pick up new scheduling and ATS tools quickly.
How do you stay organized juggling many: I lean on systems, not memory. With four years of hybrid and remote experience, I've relied heavily on diligent note-taking and daily planning—Notion and Google Workspace—to keep many threads straight without dropping one. I also anticipate contingencies: when I coordinate complex logistics across remote staff and stakeholders, I build in buffers and backup plans so a last-minute change doesn't derail the day. That combination—a reliable system plus thinking one step ahead—is what lets me run 20+ programs at once and still be the dependable point of contact people count on.
Tell us about yourself / anything else: I'm an analytical organizer with a growth mindset, grounded in an MS in Applied Organizational Psychology from USC. In practice that means I diagnose with data where a process breaks down, collaborate cross-functionally on the fix, and track results to make sure it holds. I scored 5/5 on punctuality and deadlines and was named best employee of the year—the kind of reliability that matters in a coordinator role. I'd love to bring that organization and genuine people-focus to your Recruiting team.
At Samsara, we encourage employees to br: Bringing my whole self to work means leading with curiosity and genuine care for the people behind the process. I'm a 97% extrovert who loves understanding what makes teams tick, and my background is cross-cultural—I've worked across the US, Taiwan, and with a Danish company, including an office of 22 nationalities—so I'm at my best building inclusion that's real, not surface-level. I also bring an analytical, evidence-first mindset from my Organizational Psychology training: I ask the right questions, dig for root causes, and care as much about the human side as the outcome.
